#demon #entity #evil #scary #creepy Demons Path - Evil Entity Attacks Kays & Dad! I was sat on the sofa, and Dad was in his bedroom watching TV. I kept hearing noises coming from the end of the sofa near the step. Some tapping and some scratching noises. It also felt cold there too, and I held my hand out to feel the cold spot. That was the last thing I remembered until I came around on the floor. However, having since watched this video, you can see some weird shadow like demonic entity leap up from the end of the sofa and land on me! This obviously knocked me out as I have no memory of it. After this, it looks like something picked up my arm and dragged me off the sofa onto the floor. Dad, having heard me fall onto the floor and sees my hands on the floor, starts calling my name as he gets up to come and check on me. As he almost reaches the room where I am, the shadow entity leaves me and makes some weird movements across the floor and leaps onto / into dad! Similarly to what happened to me, dad too cannot remember what happened past this point. Dad falls over sideways onto all the stuff under the stairs and slides down a mountain of objects. He does come round before me and calls for help. That's my first memory since holding my hand out at the end of the sofa. We're both confused as to what happened....until we watch the video! @Girl Carnage @Gëm @jamiecottee

d: In some parts of Southeast Asia, people believe the dead can return… still wrapped in their burial cloth. The Pocong is one of the most well-known spirits in Indonesian and Malaysian folklore. According to local beliefs, it is the ghost of a person who was buried in a traditional funeral shroud and whose spirit was unable to move on. In these burials, the body is wrapped tightly in white cloth and tied at the head, neck, and feet. Legends say that if the ties are not properly released after burial, the spirit may become trapped and return as a Pocong. Because its legs are bound by the cloth, the Pocong is often described as moving by hopping rather than walking. Many people claim to have seen these white figures at night near graveyards, forests, or abandoned roads. Even though there is no scientific evidence, the legend continues to be one of the most famous paranormal stories in Southeast Asia.

El pocong es un fantasma o espíritu atrapado que pertenece al folclore y las leyendas de Indonesia y Malasia. Físicamente se le describe como un cadáver envuelto por completo en una mortaja funeraria de tela blanca, la cual está fuertemente atada con nudos sobre la cabeza, en el cuello y en los pies. Debido a que tiene las piernas totalmente amarradas y juntas, este ser no puede caminar de forma normal, por lo que se desplaza de un lugar a otro dando saltos o flotando en el aire. Su rostro a menudo se muestra pálido, demacrado o en estado de descomposición. El origen de este mito se localiza en el Sudeste Asiático, específicamente en las islas de Indonesia, con un arraigo muy fuerte en la cultura de la isla de Java. La creencia surge directamente de los rituales de entierro islámicos tradicionales de esa región, donde el cuerpo del fallecido se envuelve en sábanas blancas antes de ir a la tumba. Según la superstición local, los nudos de la mortaja deben ser desatados antes de cubrir el cuerpo con tierra para que el alma pueda partir libremente, pero si las personas los olvidan o no los quitan, el espíritu queda atrapado dentro de la tela y el cadáver se levanta para vagar por los cementerios o caminos oscuros buscando a alguien vivo que le desate los nudos para poder descansar en paz. #umamusume #pocong #random #parati #edit

d: I came across another terrifying video showing what locals believe to be a Pocong — one of the scariest ghosts in Indonesian and Malaysian folklore. The Pocong is said to be the restless spirit of someone whose burial shroud was never untied after death. In Islamic tradition, the body is wrapped tightly in white cloth, and if the ties at the head or feet aren’t released after 40 days, the soul can’t move on. That’s when the Pocong rises… hopping from grave to grave, its face pale and eyes sunken, searching for someone to help free it — or to take revenge. Some say it smells like rotting flowers before it appears. Others claim you’ll hear a faint hopping sound outside your window before it shows itself. Whether this clip is real or fake, there’s something unsettling about how it moves… Do you think this video caught a real Pocong or something else entirely? 👀 #fyp #scary #creepy #horrortok #scarytiktoks

d: Studio dibalik kesuksesan film Colony dan Salmokji buat film horror yang mengangkat budaya “nyepi” di Bali‼️🔥💯🇮🇩 #taboothesilentday #nyepithesilentday #filmhorror #indonesia #korea

Indonesian horror draws from Javanese and Sumatran folklore, Quranic dread, and a visual language built on ancestral fear that Western horror cannot replicate. It has been quietly one of the best national horror industries in the world. 1. Satan's Slaves (Pengabdi Setan, 2017): Joko Anwar's remake of the 1982 classic is about a dead mother who refuses to stay gone. The film is rooted in Islamic dread, and it understands that the most terrifying entity is the one your family trusted completely. 2. Impetigore (2019): A woman discovers her ancestral village has been waiting for her specifically. Joko Anwar does something with Javanese shadow puppet theater that makes the film feel like a curse you cannot look away from. 3. May the Devil Take You (2018): Timo Tjahjanto takes a father's spiritual debt and makes it his children's problem. It borrows structure from Sam Raimi and builds something entirely its own. 4. The Queen of Black Magic (2019): A group of adults returns to the orphanage where they were raised and finds the cruelties committed there left a residue. The reveal of what happened is brutal and earned. 5. Macabre (2009): The Mo Brothers built the template that would define Indonesian horror for a decade. A family dinner invitation becomes an extended survival nightmare that never lets you breathe. . . . #indonesianhorror #asianhorror #horrormovies #horrorlist #foreignhorror

The footage remained buried in archived records for years before being rediscovered during a review of unexplained aerial incidents. According to reports, the recovered video left researchers and scientists stunned. What begins as an ordinary helicopter survey over a remote jungle river quickly turns into something far more disturbing. Without warning, the water erupts and a colossal humanoid entity rises from below. The creature’s pale scaled skin, lifeless white eyes, elongated arms, and dark strands of wet hair give it an appearance unlike any known species. Its massive serpentine lower body churns the river beneath it while it towers beside the aircraft. Witnesses can be heard panicking as the creature fixes its gaze directly on the helicopter and slowly reaches toward the open door. The recording ends moments later. No official explanation was ever released. But after reviewing the footage, investigators agreed on one unsettling detail… The creature didn’t seem surprised by the helicopter’s presence. It looked as if it had been waiting for it. #amazonmystery #tiktokhorror #foundfootage #fyp #scaryvideos

Sa gitna ng karagatan ng Romblon, may isang bahagi na binabalot ng katatakutan at misteryo—ang Romblon Triangle. Pinaniniwalaang lubhang mapanganib ang pagdaan dito, dahil maaari kang maglaho nang walang bakas at tuluyang maging bahagi na lamang ng isang kwento. 🌊🌫️👁️ Abangan ang kwento ni Erick sa Panaghoy at alamin ang mga karanasang hindi niya malimutan—mga tagpong puno ng pangamba, sindak, at kababalaghan. Ngayong Biyernes na, 6:00 PM  sa Spotify, Apple Podcasts, at YouTube. #Panaghoy #PanaghoySeason️2 #KwentongKatatakutan #PinoyHorror #HorrorTok #RomblonTriangle #Romblon #HorrorPodcast

Reyna Manalo observed a creature from her window at approximately 10 PM, in the Pampanga Province in the Philippines. Regional documentation  identifies the creature as an Aswang — a shape-shifting entity. Reyna stated, “It was as if it was looking right into my soul with those yellow beady eyes.” The creature was standing upright outside her window and appeared to be gesturing for her to come outside. Reyna applied a traditional coconut oil preparation reported in regional documentation to have protective properties against Aswang contact. The beast retreated shortly after the oil was administered. #aswang #philippines #folklore #cryptid #paranormal

BREAKING: Winged Creature Spotted in Rural Texas Texas, USA — Residents in a quiet rural area reported seeing a terrifying winged figure perched high in an oak tree near a dirt road late at night. A shaky smartphone video shows a thin humanoid-like creature with pale gray skin and large dark leathery wings clearly visible against the moonlight. It sits rigidly on a branch, completely still as nearby streetlights flicker across the scene. Witnesses say the camera operator can be heard whispering in fear while trying to keep the creature in frame. After several seconds, the figure suddenly shifts its body and partially opens its wings, causing the branches to shake. The person filming reportedly stepped back and stopped recording immediately, saying, “That’s not any animal I know.” Authorities have not confirmed the sighting, and no physical evidence has been found, but the video continues to circulate online, fueling speculation about what was seen in the Texas treeline that night. #weirdcreature #scaryvideos  #caughtoncamera #aswang #manananggal

d: Story time Part 1: Ang Burol ng mga ENGKANTO sa INDIA o tinatawag na Witch’s Hill - Pari Tibba Story #storytime #darkbrewstories #indianhorror #horrorstory #fyp

Locals in Canlaon City, Negros Oriental, tell that the Century Tree is enchanted and inhabited by spirits or engkanto (mythical beings in Philippine folklore). According to stories passed down by elders: The tree is said to be protected by nature spirits, and anyone who disrespects it — by cutting its branches or taking its roots — may suffer misfortune or illness. Visitors often leave offerings like coins, food, or candles as a sign of respect. Some locals believe the tree serves as a gateway between the spirit world and the human world, a sacred place where nature and the divine meet. People claim to hear whispers, rustling sounds, or feel a strange energy when standing under it, even when the air is still.  #canlaon #foryoupage
I mean respect the Engkantos, I hope you do too.  #philippines #Biringan #Engkanto #mythology #lostcity #legendary #myth #mythical

d: Overnight sa Isla ng Mangkukulam Part 1

A Mangkukulam is a Filipino witch or sorcerer known for practicing kulam, a form of traditional folk magic or black magic in the Philippines. Rooted deeply in pre-colonial beliefs, the mangkukulam is feared for their ability to inflict curses, illness, misfortune, or even death often from afar using rituals, spells, and cursed objects. #mythology #mystery #myth #mangkukulam #witch

"Mangkukulam" In Philippine folklore, a mangkukulam is a person, often depicted as a witch, who practices kulam, a form of black magic or witchcraft, and can cast spells or curses on others. They are known for using rituals, potions, and even voodoo dolls to inflict harm, illness, or misfortune on their victims. While often associated with negative actions, some mangkukulam are also believed to use their powers for healing or herbal magic, similar to an albularyo (witch doctor). #Mangkukulam #Philippines #Horror #Monster #Trending #Viral #AI #Veo3 #FYP

d: happy ending. the best of the best drama. drama title: WE ARE ALL TRYING HERE total episode: 12 available on: NETFLIX genre: slice of life, psychological, melodrama, dark comedy synopsis: A story of the struggles and journey of people tormented by envy and jealousy, while trying to find their own peace. Hwang Dong Man is an aspiring director who is the only one in the famous film industry group “The Eight” that hasn’t debuted. He tries not to get caught up in feeling worthless. Byeon Eun A works as a producer for Choi Film. She’s called “The Axe” due to her sharp screenplay reviews. She hides her anger and fears inside. Park Gyeong Se is a film director. He is particularly swayed and stressed by Hwang Dong Man after his recent movie bombed at the box office. Ko Hye Jin is the CEO of Gobak Film and is married to Park Gyeong Se. She is broad-minded and steadfast enough to embrace Hwang Dong Man's long-winded talks. Hwang Jin Man is Hwang Dong Man's older brother and a former poet. d: A lot of people talk about others because it distracts them from facing themselves. Gossip can make people feel entertained, included, or temporarily better about their own problems. But in the long run, people who constantly talk about others rarely build anything meaningful for themselves. Real growth comes when you focus less on people’s lives and more on your own purpose, peace, and progress. Not every opinion deserves your attention, and not every conversation deserves your energy.

d: LADIES! don’t let anyone (especially men) tell you not to “gossip”. often times “gossip” is how important information about who we can and cannot trust in our communities is passed along. in a time where information is abundant, but not all of it is correct, please lean into TALKING IN YOUR COMMUNITIES about things other than politics/candidates. there is meaningful change to be made within us and our communities, and “gossip” is often how word is spread.  #gossip #women #men #community #conversation

Alchemy, astronomy, and esoteric philosophy are vital components of surrealist artist Remedios Varo’s work. It is even said that she scratched the surface of masonite boards before painting on them to imbue her art with magic from the very beginning. 💎🔮 Varo’s painting La llamada (The Call) (1961) can be found at the National Museum of Women in the Arts in Washington D.C. and exemplifies her characteristic color palette with a central female figure, illuminated in fiery orange-gold tones, walking through shadowy, more muted surroundings. @NMWA  #WomenArtists #WomensHistoryMonth #ArtHistory #RemediosVaro

Wow this had me very invested the whole time, my heart was pounding from the tension between those 2. I haven't seen a BL that actually demonstrates tension and yearning in a good while so this felt like a gem. I will say the whole age gap situation is a little weird, I still don't know if the teacher liked the student while he was in high school or not, and the story seemed a bit confusing at first though I think I get it slightly more now. But regardless I don't really have anything negative to say about this short series, I really enjoyed it. I feel like if it had been a good 8 episodes all being around 30 minutes long, more characters, more depth to the story and more slow burn, this would've been a good 10/10 series. But with what we had, it was pretty damn solid. Overall 8.5/10 #fyp #BL #loveinprotocol #kbl #LGBTQ

Batangas Vlog! 🐶🐾 Ang munting bakasyon ng furbebi na may altapresyon 😝 #dogsoftiktok #minivlog  ‼️ Hello mga bes!! May ika-clarify lang po kami para iwas confusion na din sa mga followers namin na naengganyo magbook sa place. May naoverlooked kami sa rules and regulations dito  during ng stay namin. At yun ay bawal po ang PET sa BED. Small dogs lang din po ang allowed sa loob ng cottage (in case may furbaby kayo na gusto isama sa check in na medium - large breed) It was an honest mistake sa side namin mga bes kasi kung nabasa namin yun prior ng check in ay hindi kami tutuloy dahil katabi namin talaga mag sleep sina Chopper and Kaidae. Due to intense excitement ay hindi namin nabasa lahat yung rules and regulations under 'other policies' na sinend prior ng booking.  Based on our experience kasi sa mga past bookings namin sa mga pet friendly staycation ay wala restriction sa pag akyat ng pet sa bed. Gayunpaman, eto pa rin ay pagkakamali sa side namin. 🥹 Dinelete namin ang raw video ng pag-akyat ni Chopper and Kaidae sa higaan para hindi na gayahin ng ibang furparents na binabalak isama ang kanilang furbabies. The management is kind enough to let us keep this vlog bilang memories ng staycation namin with our furbabies. We genuinely apologize for the mistake. 🙏 🐾

COOL ASPIN ALERT! 🐾 Met Arthur one breezy afternoon, just casually enjoying life like the king he is. At first glance, I thought he was a Border Collie — that coat, that poise — but surprise: he’s an Aspin! (Asong Pinoy, for the unfamiliar — aka a true mixed-breed gem 🇵🇭). Arthur’s not just handsome — he’s friendly, calm, and full of character. At 12 years old, he’s aging like fine wine. Oh, and his favorite snack? Any kind of bread. 🥖😂 It’s clear Ren has given him the best life. The bond between them says it all. Thanks for letting me capture a piece of your story. Video latihan tari di Sanggar Pradnya Swari, Jembrana, Bali, viral di media sosial. Anak-anak yang berlatih—baik laki-laki maupun perempuan—tampak serius menguasai nyeledet, gerak mata khas dalam tari Bali. Nyeledet bukan sekadar melirik, tetapi gerakan cepat dan tajam yang harus selaras dengan ekspresi wajah, posisi kepala, dan irama gamelan. Teknik ini dikenal sulit karena membutuhkan konsentrasi tinggi serta kontrol otot mata yang kuat. Merujuk kajian seniman dan akademisi tari Bali, I Wayan Dibia, nyeledet menjadi elemen penting dalam membangun karakter tarian seperti Legong dan Kebyar, sekaligus menunjukkan kedalaman ekspresi penari. Dalam video yang beredar, anak-anak tampak berulang kali mengulang gerakan demi mencapai ketepatan. Latihan intens itu menggambarkan proses panjang di balik keindahan tari Bali yang selama ini terlihat memukau di atas panggung. #BALI #videoviral #tari

Keindahan setiap gerakan tidak hanya sekadar hiburan, tetapi juga menyimpan makna, nilai, dan identitas dari masing-masing daerah. 🌺 Lewat penampilan ini, aku ingin membuktikan bahwa generasi muda mampu terus melestarikan budaya sambil mengembangkan potensi diri. 🌟 Harapannya, apa yang aku bawakan bisa menjadi pengingat sekaligus inspirasi untuk kita semua agar terus menjaga dan membanggakan budaya Indonesia. 🔥 Dengan penuh rasa bangga, inilah caraku mengekspresikan talenta sekaligus membawa budaya ke atas panggung. ✨ @momoto foto studio  @GADISMagz  #TalentaGADIS2026 #talentagadis2026

The Traditional Dance of Bali: Legong Explained Legong is one of Bali’s most iconic and sacred traditional dances, known for its intricate eye movements, graceful hand gestures, and deep cultural meaning. Unlike modern dance trends, Legong requires years of training, often starting as early as five years old. Originally performed by young girls in royal courts, the dance symbolizes purity, discipline, and spiritual devotion—often as an offering to the gods during temple ceremonies. Today, men are also learning Legong, helping to preserve and evolve this art form for future generations. Every movement in Legong tells a story, usually drawn from ancient Hindu epics, making it more than just a dance—it’s a living expression of Balinese identity and heritage.  #bali #culture #traditionaldance #traditional #culturalheritage #dance #folkdance #culturaldiversitycostume #shorts #interestingshorts #uniqueshorts

Tinikling performance at Ambassador in Paradise Resort by the Boracay Folkloric Dance Collective 🇵🇭✨   Once honored as the Philippines’ national dance, Tinikling beautifully imitates the swift and graceful movements of the “tikling” birds as they glide through grass stems and skillfully dodge bamboo traps set by rice farmers.   A vibrant celebration of culture, rhythm, and tradition. 🎶🌾   #Tinikling #FilipinoCulture #Boracay #PhilippineDance #culturalheritage

Ide masak daging qurban yang paling seger! 🥩 Tanpa santan, ga pake ribet, dan dagingnya udah pasti empuk 😍😘 SOP DAGING Bahan 1: • 400 Gr Daging Sapi • 1.500 ml Air • 2½ SDT Garam Bahan 2: • 2 Batang Seledri (rajang kasar) • 2 Batang Daun Bawang Prei (potong 1 cm) • ¼ Biji Pala (bila suka) • 2 Buah Kentang • 1 Buah Wortel Bahan 3: • 75 Gr Buncis • 75 Gr Kol • 2 SDT Desaku bawang putih bubuk • 1 Sachet Ladaku merica bubuk • 2 SDM Bawang Goreng Cara Memasak: 1. Masukkan daging, air dan garam ke dalam panci. Rebus dengan api sedang sampai mendidih, kemudian buang busanya. 2. Lanjutkan merebus dengan panci tertutup sampai 3/4 matang (+/- 60 menit). 3. Masukkan Daun Bawang, Seledri, Pala (bila suka), Kentang dan Wortel masak sampai daging empuk (+/- 30 menit). 4. Masukkan buncis, kol, Desaku Bawang Putih Bubuk dan Ladaku Merica Bubuk. Masak hingga matang. 5. Sajikan dengan Bawang Goreng atau sesuai selera. #resepkurban #iduladha #lebaranhaji #tipsdagingempuk

NASI GANDUL 💕 A specialty dish from Pati, Central Java - where my Indonesian grandma is from! This is top-tier street food.  The vendor takes a plate lined with banana leaf, adds hot rice, scissor cuts meat/egg/anything you want on top of the rice, then pours this incredible rich, aromatic, sweet-savory-spicy coconut milk+beef broth soup all over.  SO FREAKIN’ GOOD!!  It’s rare to find Nasi Gandul outside of its originating region, but if you do come across it - try it 😍 #indonesianfood #nasigandul #centraljava #TheMainIngredient #MakanDenganMakna

This place has a story that just makes the food taste even better. Ayam Bakar 7 Saudara has been run by the sweetest Indonesian couple for 11 years, and every single dish reflects the decades of passion and care they’ve poured into this place. They’ve been featured twice in the Good Food Guide and after eating here, it’s not hard to see why. I ordered the grilled chicken spatchcock ($28.50) It was smokey, tender and beautifully marinated with that classic Indonesian depth of flavour. The kind of dish that reminds you what real cooking tastes like. I also grabbed a Bakso Goreng Ayam, a fried chicken meatball ($6.00) that was crispy on the outside, juicy and flavourful inside. It wasn’t featured in the video but I highly recommend it!  And then came the surprise. They brought out a complimentary dessert: a jelly-like fruit served with ice, and honestly it was the most perfect ending. After all that chilli I put on the chicken, a cool dessert was a welcome addition. This kind of hospitality just says everything about who these people are. Please go support them 🙌 📍 Ayam Bakar 7 Saudara 34 Penshurst St, Penshurst NSW 2222 Operating hours: Tuesday – Friday: 11am – 3pm, 5pm – 9pm Saturday: 11am – 9pm Sunday: Noon – 9pm Monday: Closed 📞 0414 420 988 🌐 yumbojumbo.com.au #pov #smallbusiness #Indonesia  #indonesianfood #nocustomers

Nasi Goreng Indonesia - Indonesian Fried Rice #friedrice #easyrecipes #recipe  Ingredients 3 cloves garlic, minced 1 egg 3 cups cooked rice 4 slices fish cake, thinly sliced 4 large prawns, peeled and deveined A handful of fresh squid, cleaned and sliced 1 tbsp light soy sauce 2 tbsp kecap manis (sweet soy sauce) 1 tsp dark caramel sauce 1 tbsp sambal (adjust to taste) Large pinch of YumYum or MSG 1 bird’s eye chili, finely sliced 1 spring onion, thinly sliced 2 tbsp fried garlic Sliced cucumber and tomato, to serve Fried egg, to serve INSTRUCTIONS 1. Heat a wok over high heat and add a splash of oil. Add the minced garlic and stir-fry until fragrant. 2. Crack in the egg and scramble it quickly, breaking it into small pieces as it cooks. 3. Add the cooked rice to the wok and toss thoroughly, breaking up any clumps and ensuring it’s well-coated in the egg and garlic mixture. 4. Add the fish cake, prawns, and squid. Toss everything together and stir-fry until the seafood is cooked through. 5. Mix in the light soy sauce, kecap manis, dark caramel sauce, and sambal. Stir well to evenly coat the rice and proteins with the sauce. 6. Toss in the bird’s eye chili and sliced spring onion. Stir-fry briefly to combine. 7. Sprinkle the fried garlic over the rice and give it one final toss. 8. Serve hot, garnished with sliced cucumber and tomato, and top with a fried egg.

d: Have you guys tried North Maluku food?? 😱🇮🇩 I tried it for the first time at @Ubud Food Festival in a session with @kagounga.id and it was AMAZING. 🙌🏼 That Gohu Tuna & Popeda with Ikan Kuah Kuning are my two faves from everything I tried! Unlike Nasi Goreng or Sate Ayam, Indonesian dishes from North Maluku are not as well-known & easily available across the country. Which is why the initiative from @kagounga.id to sell instant yet preservative-free packets of Popeda & Kuah Kuning is so admirable, to introduce people to North Maluku’s culinary culture by making it accessible! There’s SO MUCH to Indonesian cuisine y’all 🥹  For a country with over 17,000 islands, over 280million people and 700 indigenous & regional languages… Can you imagine how different the food culture and flavors are from region to region?  #indonesianfood #TheMainIngredient #MakanDenganMakna #uff26 #foodfestontiktok

🍗 Ayam tuh potensinya banyak! Jangan digoreng terussss ❌🙅‍♂️ Jangan sampe potensi ayam sia-sia! Sekali-sekali, coba deh kalo punya ayam dibikin jadi ayam palekko khas Bugis gini.. Pedas, gurih, medoknya pasti sukses ngabisin beras 3 karung 😅🤣 emang deh, masakan Indonesia ga ada matinya! AYAM PALEKKO Bahan 1: • 500 Gr Ayam (potong 20 bagian) • 1 SDT Desaku kunyit bubuk • 1 ½ SDT Garam • 1 SDM Air Bahan 2: (blender/haluskan) • 6 Buah Bawang Merah • 3 Buah Kemiri (goreng) • 3 Cm Jahe • 3 Cm Lengkuas • 1 SDM Desaku cabe bubuk • 1 Batang Serai (ambil 10 cm bagian putihnya saja dan rajang • 3 SDM Air Bahan 3: • 5 SDM Minyak Goreng (untuk menumis) • 3 Lembar Daun Jeruk • 2 Lembar Daun Salam Bahan 4: • 250 ml Air • 2 Kelereng Asam Jawa (rendam dengan 3 sdm air panas, ambil airnya) • 2 SDT Desaku bawang putih bubuk • ½ SDT Ladaku merica bubuk • 1 SDT Gula Cara Memasak: 1. Campurkan semua Bahan 1 ke dalam kontainer. Kocok hingga tercampur rata. Diamkan selama 30 menit. 2. Blender Bahan 2, sampai halus. 3. Panaskan 5 sendok makan Minyak, tumis bumbu yang sudah diblender (Bahan 2) sampai naik minyak. 4. Tambahkan Daun Jeruk dan Daun Salam, tumis sampai mengeluarkan aroma. 5. Masukkan ayam, tumis sampai ayam berubah warna. 6. Masukkan Bahan 4, masak sampai mendidih. 7. Kecilkan api dan masak selama 10 menit dengan wajan tertutup. 8. Lanjutkan memasak dengan wajan terbuka sampai mulai berminyak dan kekentalan kuah yang diinginkan 9. Angkat dan hidangkan. #makassar #masakanrumahan #indonesianfood

Ayam bakar seenak ini cuma pake 3 bumbu Bahan 1: 500 gr ayam 1/2 sachet desaku marinasi 1 sachet ladaku merica 3 sdm kecap manis 250ml air Bahan 2 : (campuran bumbu olesan) 2 sdm margarin 1 sachet desaku bawang putih Sisa bumbu Cara memasak: 1. masukan semua bahan 1 kedalam wajan 2. ungkep dengan api sedang selama 12 menit 3. Angkat ayam dan sisihkan 4. campurkan bahan 2 untuk bumbu olesan 5. Bakar ayam sambil diolesi dengan bumbu olesan  Selamat mencoba #resepsimpel #resepmakanannusantara #ayambakar #resepayambakar #masakansimple

DADAR JAGUNG !!!!  Asli ini salah satu gorengan yang aku ga bisa nolak si , mau dimakan langsung buat camilan enak , mau dimakan pake nasi juga mantap apa lagi dikasih sup bening bayam 🥹 udah paling cocok dah kek nya 🤣 Klo dari aku baca2 , Bakwan jagung itu yg full crispy , klo perkedel jagung / dadar jagung itu yg tengah nya masih agak lembut kayak gini , bener ga si ? 🤔 atau semua sama aja tergantung daerah ? Haha  INGREDIENTS :  - 3 pcs Jagung ( 2 di Blender , 1 Utuh ) - 4 pcs Bawang Merah - 3 pcs Bawang Putih - 2 pcs Cabe Merah Besar ( Buang biji ) - 5 pcs Daun Jeruk ( Buang tangkai ) - 1 pcs Telur - 1 sdt Garam - 2 sdt Kaldu Jamur - 1 sdt Gula - 1 sdt Lada Putih - 3 sdm Tepung Beras - 2 sdm Tepung Protein Sedang - Daun Bawang STEP : 1. Haluskan semua bahan jadi satu kecuali 1/3 Jagung dan Daun Bawang 2. Setelah semua bahan halus , masukan 1/3 jagung yg masih utuh dan daun bawang ,aduk rata 3. Goreng dengan api sedang , balik 1 x aja biar ga nyerap minyak dan ga hancur  SELESAI !!!!! Asli buat nya gampang ini dan pasti semua pada doyan klo dadar jagung mah 😍🔥  - #PyongMasak #PyongRecipe #ResepViral #MenuViral #ResepTiktok #MenuTiktok #ResepSimpel #ResepRumahan #Masakanrumahan #ResepNusantara #fyp #dadarjagung #perkedeljagung #bakwanjagung #resepjagung

Resep Sop Daging Sapi Bahan 1: 400 gr daging sapi 1.500 ml air 2½ sdt garam Bahan 2: 2 batang seledri (rajang kasar) 2 batang daun bawang prei (potong 1 cm) ¼ biji pala (bila suka) 2 buah kentang 1 buah wortel Bahan 3: 75 gr kol 2 sdt Desaku bawang putih bubuk 1 sachet Ladaku merica bubuk 2 sdm bawang goreng Cara Memasak: 1. Masukkan daging, air, dan garam ke dalam panci. Rebus dengan api sedang sampai mendidih, kemudian buang busanya. 2. Lanjutkan merebus dengan panci tertutup sampai ¾ matang (±60 menit). 3. Masukkan daun bawang, seledri, pala (bila suka), kentang, dan wortel. Masak sampai daging empuk (±30 menit). 4. Masukkan kol, Desaku bawang putih bubuk, dan Ladaku merica bubuk. Masak hingga matang. 5. Sajikan dengan bawang goreng atau sesuai selera. Catatan: Karena sop ini bumbunya simpel dan tanpa bumbu ulek, aku saranin pakai daging yang fresh ya. Kalau bisa beli yang kualitasnya bagus dari supermarket, jadi rasa gurih alami dagingnya lebih keluar #SopDaging #ResepSopDaging OlahanDaging

d: Mac n cheese with garlic butter steak bites 🤤 RECIPE⬇️ Mac n cheese: * 12 oz pasta * Chicken bouillon (for boiling pasta) * 10 oz sharp white cheddar * 8 oz Colby Jack * 5-6 oz Mozzarella  * 2 oz Parmesan * 3 tbs butter * 3 tbs flour * 1 cup heavy cream (1.5 if you prefer saucier) * 1 12-oz can evaporated milk * 1 tsp Dijon mustard * 1 tsp garlic powder * 1 tsp paprika * Salt and pepper to taste Garlic butter steak bites: * 1 lb steak, cut into bite size cubes  * 1 tsp each: garlic powder, smoked paprika, Worcestershire * 1/2 tsp Montreal steak seasoning * 1/2 tsp dried thyme * Drizzle of oil  * Salt and pepper to taste * 1/2 stick butter * 1/4 small shallot, minced * 4-5 garlic cloves, minced * Optional: chopped parsley for garnish 1. In a bowl, mix the steak with some oil and all the seasonings. Let that marinate while you prep the mac. 2. Shred cheeses and mix together. Set aside. 3. Boil pasta in water that has been generously seasoned with chicken bouillon or salt until al dente, 1-2 min below box instructions. 4. Melt butter and bloom the spices - garlic powder, paprika - for 1 min. Then add the flour and cook for a 1-2 mins. 5. While whisking, slowly add in the heavy cream and evaporated milk. Whisk until smooth.  6. Once the mixture warms up again (but NOT simmering) turn off the heat and stir in half of your cheese mixture in batches until combined. 7. Layer in a baking dish, starting with half the mac mixture, half of the leftover cheese, the rest of the mac, and finally the rest of the cheese. Sprinkle some paprika on top for color. 8. Bake at 375F for 25-30 min until golden. Broil on low for a few min for more crust if desired. 9. While the mac is baking, add oil to a pan and sear the steak until desired doneness and it develops a nice crust. 10. 1 min before the steak is done, add 1/2 stick butter, garlic, and shallots and cook for the remaining time. 11. When mac is done baking, rest 10 min before serving and top with the steak bites. #macandcheese #steakbites #garlicbutter #creamypasta

GIRIK 🥟  Gerekli malzemeler  Hamur için: •3 su bardağı un •yarım tatlı kaşığı tuz •aldığı kadar su (yaklaşık 1,5 su bardağı kullandım ben una göre değişebilir kontrollü ilave edin) İç harcı için: •2 su bardağı pirinç •2 parça tavuk göğsü •1 yemek kaşığı tereyağı •1 yemek kaşığı salça •1 tatlı kaşığı tuz •1 tatlı kaşığı pul biber •1 tatlı kaşığı toz biber •1 çay kaşığı karabiber •üzerini bir parmak geçecek kadar su ya da tavuk suyu Üzeri için: •2 yemek kaşığı tereyağı •4 yemek kaşığı sıvı yağ •1 yemek kaşığı salça Banmak ya da üstüne dökmek için: •yoğurt (ben sarımsaklı yaptım çok daha iyi oldu) Yapılışı İç harcı için tavukları haşlayalım. İnce ince didikleyelim. Pilav tenceresine tereyağı ve salçayı alıp kavuralım. Pirinci ve baharatları üzerine ilave edip biraz daha kavuralım. Tavukları da ekleyip karıştıralım. Üzerini bir parmak geçecek kadar su veya tavuk suyu ilave edip pişmeye bırakalım. O sırada hamuru hazırlayalım. Un su ve tuzu ele yapışmayacak bi hamur elde edene kadar yoğuralım. Minik bezeler yapıp üzerini örtüp dinlenmeye bırakalım. Pilav piştikten sonra el yakmayacak kadar soğumasını bekleyelim. Pilavı avuç avuç porsiyonlara bölelim. Bezeleri çok ince olmayacak şekilde açalım. Ortasına pilavdan koyup kapatalım. Fazlalık hamuru koparalım. Hepsini bu şekilde yaptıktan sonra kaynar suda 6-7 dakika kadar haşlayalım. İsterseniz benim gibi sunum yapabilirsiniz isterseniz de bir tabağa iki üç tane koyup üzerine yoğurt ve yağ gezdirebilirsiniz. d: Lemongrass chicken with sugar snaps 🫛🫚 You can find the full written recipe on my Recipe Portal 🙌 Macros per serve: 36g protein 30g fat 68g carbs 684 calories per serve 💪  Ingredients for 3 servings: For the chicken 🍗  5 Chicken Thigh Fillets (Skin-On)  3cm Fresh Ginger 3 Cloves of Garlic 1 Stalk of Lemongrass 1 Tbsp Brown Sugar Small Handful of Coriander 1 Tsp Chilli Flakes 1 Tbsp Dark Soy Sauce 1.5 Tbsp Mayonnaise 1 Tbsp Olive Oil For the sugar snaps 🌶️  200g Sugar Snaps 1 Tsp Chilli Crisp Oil 1/2 Fresh Lime 1 Tbsp Soy Sauce Serve with 🍚  200g Uncooked Rice Cooking instructions 👨‍🍳  Oven: Bake skin-side up at 220°C fan for 35/40 mins until the skin is caramelised and crispy. Air fryer: Cook skin-side up at 200°C for 18/22 mins until crispy. #highprotein #easyrecipes #mealprep #healthyfood #dinnerideas

d: Home Cooking Sessions: Thai Beef Salad with the most incredible lemongrass dressing. Full recipe and long-form video now up on Substack, link in bio 🇹🇭😍 - For me, there are very few salads that compare to this one, and a lot of that comes down to the dressing. It’s sharp, sweet, salty and spicy, with layers of heat, citrus and umami that somehow all work together perfectly. It’s incredibly versatile too, so once you make the dressing, you can pair it with all sorts of proteins.  I used lemongrass in both the dressing and butter baste to bring freshness and that really fragrant citrus note through the beef. The toasted rice powder is another key element here - it adds nuttiness, texture and body whilst helping absorb excess moisture so the salad stays crisp. There’s plenty of crushing, grinding and layering of flavours in this one, but if you don’t have a mortar and pestle, a food processor works perfectly well too. Full recipe, ingredients and long-form video now live on Substack, link in bio. NF x

d: ＜材料＞ ・豚バラ肉…200g ・人参…1/2本 ・椎茸…お好みの量 ・小松菜…適量 ・乾燥ひじき…大さじ2 ・米…2合 ◎水…100ml ◎砂糖…大さじ1 ◎みりん…大きじ1 ◎酒…大さじ1 ◎醤油…大さじ2 ◎オイスターソース…大さじ2 ＜作り方＞ 1.乾燥ひじきは水で戻しておく 2.人参を千切り、豚肉を一口大に切る 3.フライパンにごま油をひき、豚肉を炒める 4.色が変わってきたら、人参と椎茸、ひじきを加え全体に油を馴染ませる 5.◎の調味料と小松菜を加え蓋をし弱火で10分煮る 6.洗った米に煮汁と2合目まで水をいれ通常炊飯 7.炊き上がったら具材を加え混ぜれば完成！ #おうちごはん#炊き込みご飯
h: おうちごはん, 炊き込みご飯

d: Greek plate 🇬🇷 Chicken fillet marinated in olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, salt & pepper. Boiled potatoes finished in butter until golden and crispy. Fresh salad with tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, parsley, olive oil & salt. Tzatziki: 1 small cucumber 200 g yogurt 2 garlic cloves Dill 2 tbsp olive oil 1 tbsp wine vinegar Salt & pepper Mix everything for the tzatziki and let it chill before serving 🤍 Plato griego 🇬🇷 Filete de pollo marinado en aceite de oliva, jugo de limón, orégano, sal y pimienta. Patatas cocidas y luego doradas en mantequilla hasta que queden crujientes. Ensalada fresca de tomate, pepino, cebolla morada, perejil, aceite de oliva y sal. Tzatziki: 1 pepino pequeño 200 g de yogur 2 dientes de ajo Eneldo 2 cdas de aceite de oliva 1 cda de vinagre de vino Sal y pimienta Mezcla todos los ingredientes del tzatziki y deja enfriar antes de servir 🤍 #greekfood #greekplate #easyrecipes #homemadefood #foodblogger

ทำเมนูแกงเส้นไก่อีกแล้ว กินช่วงฝนตกนี่ แซบบบ น้ำเปล่า 3 ลิตร ซุปผง 3 ช้อน กระเทียม 3 กลีบ รากผักชี 4 ราก น้ำตาล 2 ช้อน ซอสฝาเขียว 3 ช้อน ซอสหอย 2 ช้อน ซีอิ๊วดำ 2 ช้อน น้ำกระเทียมดอง+เนื้อ 5 ช้อน  โครงไก่ 400 กรัม อกไก่ 200 กรัม ตีนไก่ 500 กรัม ปีกไก่ 250 กรัม เลือดไก่ ตามชอบ หมูยอ ตามชอบ ถั่วงอก  วุ้นเส้น  *ช้อน คือช้อนแกงก้านสั้นตามที่เอื้อยใช้ในคลิปเด้อจ้า  #เอื้อยเก๋ #ต้มเส้น #วุ้นเส้น #ทำอาหาร #อาหารอีสาน #อาหารอร่อย #อาหารอร่อย #ห้องครัวtiktok #tiktokพากิน #เมนูดังtiktok #เอื้อยเก๋ติดครัว #tiktokuni

d: Real cheeseburger in Tokyo. A halved cheeseburger is placed right on top of bubbling hot cheese sauce on the griddle—an unusual and eye-catching style ✨ Created by a true burger enthusiast who studied burgers around the world, this menu delivers deep cheese flavor while a spicy sauce inside keeps it surprisingly balanced and not too heavy. Clean, satisfying, and easy to enjoy. Sit at the counter and you can watch the entire burger-making process right in front of you, which makes it even more fun. If you’re looking for something a little different during your Tokyo trip, this spot is worth a stop. [American Diner Andra]  - Location: 5-13-7 Higashiueno, Taito City, Tokyo - Hours: 11:30–14:00 / 18:00–22:00 (until 21:00 on weekends) - Menu: Touchdown Burger Triple (¥2,700) #cheeseburger #burger #tokyofood #tokyotravel

d: Creamy Tomato Pasta with Wagyu Steak 🍝 🥩  Ingredients:                                      •Olive oil and butter • 1 Ib pasta of choice • 2 shallots, finely chopped • 5 garlic cloves, finely chopped • 2 tbsp tomato paste • 1 cup heavy cream • ½-1 cup pasta water • 1 cup freshly grated Parmigiano Reggiano, plus more for serving • Fresh basil for garnish • 1 wagyu steak • 1 tbsp olive oil • Salt & pepper to taste • 1 tsp chili flakes • 1 tsp Italian seasoning Method: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pasta until al dente. Reserve about 1 cup of pasta water before draining. In a large pan over medium heat, add the olive oil, shallots, and garlic. Sauté until soft and fragrant. Stir in the tomato paste and let it cook for 2-3 minutes until rich and deep in color. Season with salt, pepper, chili flakes, and Italian seasoning. Pour in the heavy cream and mix until smooth. Add about ½ cup pasta water and stir. Add the grated Parmigiano Reggiano and mix until the sauce becomes creamy and silky. Add the cooked pasta directly into the sauce and toss until fully coated. Add more pasta water as needed to loosen the sauce. Season the wagyu steak with salt, pepper, and a touch of olive oil. Sear in a hot pan for a few minutes per side until beautifully caramelized and cooked to your liking. Let rest, then slice. Serve the pasta in bowls and top with sliced wagyu steak, fresh basil, and extra Parmigiano Reggiano. #wagyu #steak #pasta #food #asmr

Spicy Cucumber & Carrot Salad 🥒🥕✨ Fresh, crunchy, tangy, and a little spicy. This easy cucumber and carrot salad is packed with flavor and makes the perfect refreshing side dish. Serves 4 Ingredients * 2 cucumbers * 2 carrots * 1/2 onion, thinly sliced * 3 green onions, sliced * 2–3 garlic cloves, minced * 1 tbsp chili powder or gochugaru * 1 tsp sugar * 1 tsp MSG or 1–2 tbsp soy sauce * 2 tbsp oil * 1 tbsp vinegar * Salt, to taste Instructions 1. Thinly slice the cucumbers and carrots using a mandoline. Add salt and let them sit for 10–15 minutes to soften slightly. Lightly squeeze out excess water if needed. 2. Add the onion, green onions, garlic, chili powder, sugar, MSG or soy sauce, and vinegar. 3. Heat the oil until very hot, then carefully pour it over the garlic and seasoning to release the aroma. 4. Mix everything well together and adjust salt if needed. 5. Serve immediately as a side dish or chill for 20–30 minutes for even better flavor. ✨ Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3–4 days.#vegetariandelight #asian #spicy #cucumbersalad #sidedish

d: 他のレシピ▷@eri.gohan_ そうめんをラーメン風にアレンジした冷やしそうラーメンを作ってみたよ✨ スープは身近な調味料混ぜたらできるし 薄切り肉と調味料合わせてレンチンするだけで チャーシュー風のトッピングまで作れるから 実はお手軽レシピ🤭 なのに味はとっても本格的だよ❤️ ぜひ作ってみてね☺️ === ◎材料（2人分） ・そうめん　　　　　　　　150〜200g ・薄切り豚肉　　　　　　　200g （豚肉の調味料） ・しょうゆ　　　　　　　　大さじ1.5 ・酒　　　　　　　　　　　大さじ1 ・みりん　　　　　　　　　大さじ1 ・砂糖　　　　　　　　　　大さじ1 ・片栗粉　　　　　　　　　小さじ2 （スープ調味料） ・しょうゆ　　　　　　　　大さじ3 ・めんつゆ（2倍濃縮）　　　大さじ2 ・鶏ガラスープの素　　　　小さじ2 ・ごま油　　　　　　　　　小さじ2 ・酢　　　　　　　　　　　小さじ1 ・水　　　　　　　　　　　600mL （トッピング） ・ゆで卵　　　　　　　　　好きなだけ ・のり　　　　　　　　　　好きなだけ ・ねぎ　　　　　　　　　　お好みで ・黒こしょう　　　　　　　お好みで ◎作り方 ①スープの調味料を合わせて冷蔵庫で冷やしておく。 ②耐熱皿に豚肉と豚肉用の調味料を入れてよく混ぜ、ラップをし600Wのレンジで3分ほど加熱する。レンチンが終わったら全体を混ぜ、冷ましておく。 ③器にゆでたそうめん、スープ、氷2〜3個を入れる。豚肉、ゆで卵、のり、ねぎ、黒こしょうをトッピングしたら完成！ ※そうめんと氷を入れるとスープが若干薄まるから、少し濃いめに作るのがポイント。水や氷の量でお好みの濃さに調節してね！ === このアカウントでは 夫が喜ぶ簡単レシピを発信しています💌 ⚪︎家族が喜ぶごはんを作りたい ⚪︎毎日のごはんをできるだけ簡単に作りたい ⚪︎料理のレパートリーを増やしたい そんな方にぴったりなレシピを投稿していくので 気になる人はフォローしてね🩷 #簡単ごはん #おうちごはん #そうめん

This might be the only soup I will never get tired of eating 🥰 Beef Sinigang is so comforting and delicious!  Ingredients 2 packets @knorr tamarind soup mix 1 lb beef brisket 3 beef spare ribs Fish sauce to taste 3 tomatoes 1 large onion 2 taro root Handful of okra 1 eggplant 6 bundles bok choy Handful of green beans 3 Thai chili Salt & pepper to taste Instructions Season the brisket with salt, pepper, and fish sauce. Sear with 1 tomato, 1/2 onion, and taro until browned. Cover with water and pressure cook for 45 minutes. Add remaining veggies + tamarind soup mix. Boil until veggies are tender (not mushy). Serve with rice and enjoy. #sinigang #beefsinigang #soursoup #filipinofood #pinoyfood

🇵🇭 Kare Kare | Filipino Food Bible Ep. 10 Kare-kare is a rich Filipino stew made with oxtail, tripe, and vegetables, all simmered in a thick peanut sauce. It’s usually served with bagoong (fermented shrimp paste) on the side to balance the flavors and an extra level of umami and saltiness 😋 INGREDIENTS: Boiling liquid: - 1-2 red onions, chopped - 2 bulbs of garlic, chopped - 1 tbsp of salt - 1 tbsp peppercorns - 5-6 bay leaves - 1 kg of oxtail - 650g of ox tripe - Beef stock as needed Kare Kare: - 10g of annatto powder - 2 tablespoons oil - 15-20 cloves garlic, minced - 2 tbsp annatto powder - 1 white onion, diced - 1 banana heart, chopped, washed and rinsed w/ salt - Beef stock as needed - green beans, chopped - eggplants, quartered - pechay/bok choy - 1 ½ cup peanut butter - Patis to taste - ½ teaspoon salt - 1 tbsp of sugar (optional to taste) Toasted rice slurry: - 1:5 tbsp of water to toasted rice, blended Served with:  - Bagoong  - Crushed peanuts INSTRUCTIONS:  - Start by seasoning your meat with salt and pepper and sear. Add all ingredients under “boiling liquid” and fill the pot as needed with beef stock. Simmer until tender, remove meat and set aside the broth for later.  - Saute onions, garlic, and banana hearts in oil mixed with annatto powder. Add your meat back and cover with the broth from the boiling liquid and simmer for 15-20 minutes. - To thicken the mixture, add toasted rice to a food processor or blender. Take some of the rice and mix with water to form our slurry.  - Add both rice slurry and peanut butter stir and reduce to thicken. Season with patis/salt/pepper and add your vegetables.  - Serve alongside bagoong top and enjoy!  #CookingASMR #ASMR #FilipinoFood #PinoyFood

d: 🇵🇭 Pork Adobo | Filipino Food Bible – Episode 1 My take on one of the most iconic Filipino dishes - Pork Adobo! This series will feature all filipino dishes in all their variations. consider this your guide to filipino cuisine. comment what you wanna see next! INGREDIENTS: Pork Adobo - 1.2 kg pork belly (or cut of choice) - 15–20 cloves garlic, minced (adjust to taste—more is always welcome) - 1 onion, chopped - 500 ml pork broth - 190 ml soy sauce - 100 ml cane vinegar - 3 long green chilies - 4 bay leaves - 1 tbsp whole peppercorns - 30 g palm sugar, chopped (adjust to sweetness) - Oil, for frying Toppings - Sliced onion leeks - Crispy garlic bits - Boiled eggs INSTRUCTIONS - Fry the pork until lightly browned on all sides, then remove and set aside. - In the same pot, sauté the garlic until lightly golden and fragrant. Add onions and cook until softened. - Return the pork to the pot, pour in the pork broth. Add the soy sauce, vinegar, green chilies, bay leaves, peppercorns, and palm sugar.  - Cover and let simmer on medium heat for 5-10 mins **don’t mix to allow vinegar to cook.  - Turn to low heat for 1–2 hours, until the pork is tender and the sauce has reduced. you want it to be almost swimming in its own fat like a confit - Optional: If serving with eggs, add the soft boiled eggs 2-3 mins before serving to retain jammyness - Serve over hot rice, topped with crispy garlic and leeks, and a hard-boiled egg on the side. Enjoy. #FilipinoFoodBible #filipinofood #asmr #cookingasmr #pinoyfood
Filipino Food #eat #taste #turon #lumpia

🇵🇭 Suam na Mais (Filipino Corn Soup) | Soups So Good It Can Cure Any Sickness Ep. 28 Suam na mais is a  Filipino corn soup with roots in Pampanga - a province in the 🇵🇭. It’s usually made w/ white corn (malagkit na mais). My grandmother used to make this when I was younger so this holds a special place in my ❤️ It’s honestly one of the tastiest and most comforting soups I’ve ever had and it’s kinda reminiscent of a thin porridge as well 😋 INGREDIENTS:  Shrimp/Chicken Stock: - 1 kg of shrimp shells - Chicken stock as needed Suam na Mais  - 1 kg of prawn/shrimp - 8 pcs white corn, chopped and half grated - 10-15 cloves of garlic, chopped  - 1 white onion, chopped - ½ kilo chicken cuts - Shrimp/Chicken broth as needed - 1 tbsp of patis - 3-5 pcs long green chilies - ½ cup of malunggay leaves - salt and pepper to taste *measurements are just estimates. Taste and adjust w your ❤️ INSTRUCTIONS:  - remove and chop corn kernels. grate half including the cob. The natural starch will help thicken the broth. - Make a simple shrimp-chicken stock by simmering shrimp shells. Sear the shrimp meat separately. - sauté garlic and onions. Add the chicken pieces, then pour in the prepared stock. Season with fish sauce and add the reserved corn cobs. simmer for 20–30 minutes, then remove the cobs. - Add the shrimp, along with the grated and chopped corn and allow to thicken. add chilies and malunggay off heat - Serve hot and enjoy! #CookingASMR #ASMR #Soups #filipinofood #pinoyfood

Filipino Pork Adobo 🤎 Ingredients: * 2 lbs pork shoulder, cut into cubes * 2 tbsp oil * 1 large onion, sliced * 8 cloves garlic, sliced * 3 cups water * 1/2 cup soy sauce * 1 1/2 tsp dark soy sauce * 1/4 cup rice vinegar * 3 tbsp brown sugar * 1 1/2 tsp black peppercorns * 5 bay leaves * Pineapple chunks * Green onion + rice for serving Instructions: 1. Heat oil and sear pork until browned on all sides. Remove and set aside. 2. In the same pot, sauté onion for 2 minutes, then add garlic and cook until fragrant. 3. Add water, soy sauces, vinegar, brown sugar, peppercorns, and bay leaves. Stir well. 4. Return pork to the pot with all the juices. Bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer partially covered for 1 1/2 hours until the pork is fall-apart tender. 5. While simmering, caramelize pineapple chunks in a pan until golden on both sides. 6. Remove pork from the pot and boil the sauce over high heat for 7–10 minutes until slightly reduced. 7. Add pork and pineapple back into the sauce and toss gently to coat. 8. Serve over jasmine rice and top with green onions. Sweet, savory, tangy, and so comforting 🤍  #food #foodie #homecook #recipesoftiktok

🇵🇭 Pork Binagoongan | Filipino Food Bible Ep. 11 One of the more underrated but S tier Filipino dishes!! Usually made with pork simmered in “bagoong alamang”, or fermented shrimp paste - best served with rice, sometimes with eggplant or green mango on the side. Its savory, funky, and slightly sweet, with tons of umami 💡Pro tip: adjust depending on your bagoong. Not all bagoong tastes the same — some are saltier, milder, or sweeter — so taste as you go and balance it out with sugar, vinegar, and tomatoes. INGREDIENTS: Binagoongan: - 1.5 kg pork ribs, blanched (you can use pork belly or cut of choice) - 5-6 tbsp bagoong guisado - 1 large onion, diced - 15-20 cloves garlic, sliced - 4 long green chilies, sliced - 350g tomatoes, sliced - 1/3 cup cane vinegar - 1/3 cup brown sugar - 1/4 cup soy sauce - Water as needed - Black pepper, to taste Bagoong Guisado: - 400g shrimp paste (alamang) - 25-30 cloves garlic - 7-10 red chilies - 6-7 shallots - 400g tomatoes, diced - 1/4 cup vinegar - 1/3 cup brown sugar Ensaladang Mangga: - Green mango - Red onion - Cilantro - Tomato - Lime - Salt - Pepper INSTRUCTIONS:  - Blanch pork for 10 minutes, before straining and rinsing. Set aside. - To make the bagoong start by blitzing onions or shallots, garlic, and red chilies in a processor.  Add to cold pan with oil and cook until garlic is slightly oasted add tomatoes, alamang, sugar and vinegar. Allow this to simmer until the oil comes out and the tomatoes are jammy. - For the binagoongan start by sauteing onions, garlic, and chilies. add back your meat and all other ingredients listed under  “Binagoongan” and simmer for 40mins-1hr or until tender - In the meantime you can pair this with a fresh ensalada by combining all ingredients under “ensalada”. - Serve and enjoy! #CookingASMR #ASMR #FilipinoFood #pinoyfood

MANGO FLOAT 🥭🇵🇭 The best mango dessert ever created?  A simple 4 ingredient no-bake dessert from the Philippines. Mango float is inspired by the Spanish dessert known as Crema de Fruta.   Filipino cooks would use what they had readily available to replace the original Crema de Fruta ingredients and mango float was born.  Ingredients: Ripe Mangoes  Graham crackers Heavy cream Condensed milk  Instructions: 1. Slice fresh ripe mangos into a strips or cubes.  2. Add heavy cream to a big bowl and whip until you have medium peaks 3. Add condensed milk to your preference of sweetness. Taste as you go.  4. Gently fold the condense milk in, making sure not to overly deflate the whipped cream 5.  Now in your container begin assembling 6. Go whipped cream, then graham crackers, then whipped cream, then mangoes, then graham cracker, and repeat as many times as you desire.  7. Let it chill in the fridge for at least 3 hours or overnight. And enjoy #mangofloat #mango #nobake #filipino #philippines

Filipino Bistek Tagalog with extra onions 
 Bistek Marinade: 1.4 lbs (635g) thin-sliced beef (from a whole chuck roll)
 ¼ cup soy sauce
 ½ cup calamansi juice
 3–4 tbsp oyster sauce
 2–3 tbsp minced garlic
 1 tsp ground black pepper 
 For Cooking:
 2 large sweet onions, sliced into rings (the more the merrier!)
 1 head garlic, minced
 2 tbsp avocado oil
 2 tbsp brown sugar
 ¼ cup water
 2 tbsp calamansi juice (the final touch for extra tang) 
 Instructions:
 In a large bowl, combine the beef with all the marinade ingredients. Cover tightly with cling wrap and marinate in the refrigerator overnight.
 In a wok or heavy pot over medium heat, add the oil, marinated beef and its liquid. Simmer until the meat is tender. (Keep in mind: if the meat isn’t cut evenly, the thinner slices will cook faster. Set those aside early so they don’t overcook). 
 Once the meat is cooked through, remove it from the pot. Let the remaining sauce continue to simmer until it thickens and the oils begin to separate. 
 Skim some of the beef oil from the thickened sauce and use it in the same pan to sauté the minced garlic until fragrant. Toss in the sweet onion rings and cook until they are half-cooked and still crunchy. 
 Return the beef and the thickened sauce to the pan, tossing everything together until well combined. Just before turning off the heat, add the final 2 tablespoons of calamansi juice for that signature extra tang! #bistek #filipino #filipinofood

Want Pinoy food that lasts in your fridge that's NOT adobo? Try this recipe. 🔥  This version of estofado traces back to a 1935 cookbook, and the cooking process tells a story. In a time before refrigeration, it was made to last, feeding families for days. Every bite offers a glimpse into how people once cooked with care, preserving both flavor and longevity through thoughtful techniques and ingredients. 👩🏻‍🍳  ESTOFADONG FILIPINO From the book: 1935 Libro nin Pagluto asin Pagconserva Ingredients: 1 kilo of meat (usually beef or pork) 1 cup vinegar 2 onions 5 saba bananas (ripe plantains) 2 glasses of water 1 cup sugar 1 head of garlic 1 small glass (copita) of gin (Ginebra) Procedure: 1. Fry the meat until browned. Set aside the fried meat, this will be used for the stew. 2. In a separate pot or pan, caramelize the sugar by heating it with a bit of vinegar. 3. Once the sugar has melted and slightly caramelized, add the remaining vinegar and water. Stir and cook until the mixture resembles a broth. 4. While it's simmering, prepare a pot (koron) by lining the bottom with banana leaves or any barrier to prevent the meat from sticking or burning. 5. Place the fried meat into the pot, along with all the ingredients: chopped onions, garlic, sliced saba bananas, and the prepared broth. Also, add the gin at this stage. 6. Cover the meat with banana leaves to seal in moisture and flavor. 7. Cook over low heat, and once it starts boiling, reduce the fire further and let it slowly cook until the meat is tender. 8. Once done, this dish can be preserved for up to a month, and reheated only when ready to serve. #fyp #featrmedia #filipinofood #estofado

Grabe ang Sarap ng Balbacua! 🐮 Lutong Bahay na Comfort Food sa Pata ng Baka! #Balbacua #PinoyRecipe  Balbacua Recipe — rich, flavorful, and comforting Pinoy beef stew! 😋 Malambot na beef pata na niluto sa tausi at tomato sauce — perfect sa kanin 🍛 🧂 Ingredients: • 1 kg Beef Pata (Trotters)  Pakuluan sa tubig na may sibuyas, luya, at asin, pressure cook 45 mins. • 1 tbsp cooking oil • 1 thumb-size ginger (sliced) • 1 large onion (chopped) • 5 cloves garlic (minced) • 1 tbsp annatto (anato) powder • 1 small red bell pepper (sliced) • ½ cup tomato sauce • 2 tbsp tausi • 1 tsp black pepper powder • 2 pcs spicy green chili • 2 pcs siling pansigang • 1 pc broth cube • 1L water • Spring onions & toasted garlic (pang garnish) 🔥 Procedure: 1️⃣ Painitin ang mantika, igisa ang luya, bawang, at sibuyas. 2️⃣ Idagdag ang annatto powder at red bell pepper. 3️⃣ Ilagay ang pinakuluang beef pata at haluin. 4️⃣ Isama ang tomato sauce, tausi, black pepper, sili, broth cube at tubig. 5️⃣ Pakuluin hanggang lumapot at malasa. 6️⃣ Timplahan ayon sa panlasa, lagyan ng spring onions at toasted garlic. 🍛 Best served with: steamed or fried rice! #Balbacua #PinoyRecipe #FilipinoFood #LutongBahay #ComfortFood #BeefPata #Cookingtripco #Foodtok #SarapNgPinoy #HomeCooking #FoodVlog #RecipeVideo #foryou

d: Espasol is one of those snacks that always reminds you of your childhood. ❤️  Soft, chewy, and coated in toasted rice flour, it has that lightly nutty flavor that keeps you reaching for another piece. It's the kind of kakanin that feels comforting the moment you take a bite. Catch more food stories from our Laguna series on YouTube. 🔥  ESPASOL Ingredients: 16 oz glutinous rice flour 2 cans coconut milk 1 can evaporated milk 1 can condensed milk pinch salt macapuno Procedure: 1. Toast the glutinous rice flour in a large pan or wok on medium-high heat and stir frequently so it doesn’t burn. Once it turns a light shade of brown and becomes aromatic, remove from heat. Set aside about ½ cup toasted flour to coat the bottom of your pan. 2. In a large bowl, add all ingredients, including the rest of the toasted flour and stir until incorporated. Transfer back to the pan and bring to a boil.  3. Lower heat to medium-low and stir until the mixture forms into a ball.  4. Once the mixture is smooth and shiny, remove from heat and transfer to the prepared pan. Spread evenly. 5. Sprinkle the rest of the toasted flour and make sure to coat the full surface. Slice and serve! #fyp #featrmedia #filipinofood #laguna #espasol

What's your favorite kakanin? 🌾 We love rice cakes so much that we just had to make a whole series about it. This is ✨ 15 Days of Kakanin with Chef Martin ✨ We will be cooking all of your favorite kakanins in our studio using ingredients that are easily accessible and tools that might already be in your kitchen! 👨🏻‍🍳  We are kickstarting this series with the classic biko. Did you know that there are superstitions surrounding this kakanin during Undas? It is usually cooked by the elderly during this sacred holiday to remember their loved ones that have passed. 🕊️ Try this recipe at home today! 🔥 BIKO Ingredients: 2 cups glutinous rice, washed and soaked in water for 2 hours 2 cups water 1 bundle pandan leaves 1-1/2 cups brown sugar 3 cups coconut milk ½ tsp salt 2 cups coconut cream  Procedure: 1. In a pot, bring rice, water, and pandan to a boil. Simmer covered for 10 minutes or until most of the water has been absorbed. 2. Add sugar, milk, and salt. Cook over low heat while stirring until rice is fully-cooked. 3. Make latik by simmering coconut cream until reduced and strain out the oil. Top kakanin with latik. Makes 8 servings. #fyp #featrmedia #chefmartin #kakanin #biko #ricecake #filipinofood #undas

Hot Honey Chicken Pasta 🍯🔥 the viral recipe with over 60+ million views, 20/10 hits every time! Recipe: Chicken Tenders • 1 lb chicken tenderloins • 1 tsp onion powder • 1 tsp garlic powder • 1 tsp seasoned salt • 1 tsp creole seasoning • 1 tsp cayenne pepper (optional, adjust to spice) • 1 tsp all purpose seasoning • 1 sazon packet • 1–2 cups buttermilk • ⅛ cup jalapeño or pickle juice • 3 cups all-purpose flour (season with same seasonings) Hot Honey Sauce • ½ cup buffalo sauce • ¼ cup honey • ⅛ cup hot sauce (optional) Pasta • 1 lb penne pasta • 2 tbsp unsalted butter • 2 garlic cloves, finely minced • ½ yellow onion, finely chopped • 1 ½ cups heavy cream • 1 tsp onion powder • 1 tsp garlic powder • 1 tsp seasoned salt • 1 tsp creole seasoning • 1 tsp cayenne pepper (optional) • 1 tsp all purpose seasoning • 1 tsp black pepper • Grated mozzarella • Grated Parmesan Instructions 1. Season chicken tenderloins add in buttermilk and jalapeño/pickle juice then marinate for at least 30 minutes to an hour.  2. Coat chicken in seasoned flour set aside on wire rack and rest for 10 minutes before frying. Fry at 350°F until golden brown and crispy. Set aside and let cool on a wire rack. 3. In a sauce pan, add 1 tbsp of unsalted butter, buffalo sauce, honey, and hot sauce and simmer until fully combined. Then toss chicken in the hot honey sauce. 4. In a skillet, melt butter and sauté garlic and onion until fragrant. Pour in heavy cream and stir in all seasonings. 5. Add mozzarella and Parmesan until melted and creamy, then toss in cooked pasta. 6. Top creamy pasta with hot honey chicken and extra sauce if desired and enjoy :) #hothoneychickenpasta #hothoney #chickenpasta #pasta #fypシ

Recipe⤵️ Would you eat this? Honey Sesame Chicken Ingredients: For the Chicken: 1 chicken breast, cubed ½ tsp salt ½ tsp black pepper ½ tsp nutritional yeast ½ tsp baking powder 1 egg 2 tbsp cold water 1 tbsp avocado oil 2 tbsp cornstarch 2 tbsp flour (I used gluten-free) For the Sauce: ½ tbsp avocado oil 2 cloves garlic, minced 2 tbsp honey 2 tbsp soy sauce 1 tbsp ketchup ½ tbsp rice vinegar ½ tbsp toasted sesame oil 1 tbsp sesame seeds ¼ cup water + 1 cornstarch, mixed Green onions, chopped, for topping Directions: For the Chicken: In a bowl, combine the chicken, salt, black pepper, nutritional yeast, and baking powder In a separate bowl, whisk together the egg, water, avocado oil, cornstarch, and flour Pour the egg mixture over the chicken and let sit for 10-15 minutes Heat 2-3 inches of oil to 350 F, then fry the chicken pieces until at least 165 F at the thickest part Remove the chicken from the oil, then place on a paper towel to absorb excess oil For the Sauce: In a bowl, combine the honey, soy sauce, ketchup, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and sesame seeds In a wok or high-sided pan on medium-low heat, warm the oil, then cook the garlic until fragrant, around 1-2 minutes Add in the sauce mixture and the cornstarch and water mixture, then mix until thickened Remove from the heat and add in the chicken pieces; mix and serve topped with green onions Enjoy!! #sesamechicken #chinesefood #trendingfoods #viralrecipes

Recipe Name Steak Fajita Tray Bake Ingredients 1. 2 lbs flank steak, sliced 2. 3 bell peppers, sliced 3. 1 large onion, sliced 4. 2 tbsp olive oil 5. 1 tbsp chili powder 6. 1 tsp paprika 7. 1 tsp garlic powder 8. 1 tsp cumin 9. 1 tsp salt 10. 1/2 tsp black pepper 11. Warm tortillas for serving Recipe 1. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). 2. Place sliced peppers in a large tray. 3. Add sliced onion. 4. Drizzle vegetables with olive oil. 5. Season with chili powder and paprika. 6. Add garlic powder and cumin. 7. Toss vegetables to coat evenly. 8. Arrange sliced steak on top. 9. Season steak with salt and pepper. 10. Roast for 15-18 minutes. 11. Broil for 2 minutes for extra char. 12. Remove from the oven. 13. Let the steak rest briefly. 14. Toss everything together. 15. Serve with warm tortillas. These garlic Parmesan chicken wings saved me from ELIMINATION on Next Level Chef 😮‍💨🔥 -In a large bowl add 2C of buttermilk, 1 egg,  1/2tsp garlic powder,  1/2tsp onion powder,  1/2tsp smoked paprika,  1/2tsp cayenne,  1/2tsp salt,  1/2tsp pepper, 1/4C pickle juice, and 3tb hot sauce then mix -add your wings and marinate for at least 20 minutes Garlic Parm Sauce -in a large bowl mix 1/3C Mayo, 1tsp sugar, 2TB lemon juice, 1/2tsp pepper, 1/2C grated parm and mix -in a medium pan on shmedium heat melt 1/3 stick of unsalted butter and add 1/4tsp dried oregano, 1/4tsp dried thyme, 1/4tsp dried basil, 1/4tsp chili flake and swirl until aromatic -then add in 6 finely chopped garlic cloves for 40 sec- turn off heat then mix in 1 tsp of fresh chopped parsley -add this to your mayo and mix  Wing Time -fill a medium sauce pot 3/4 full of peanut oil, bring to 375F -in a bowl add 3/4c flour, 3/4c corn starch, 1tsp garlic powder, 1tsp onion powder, 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1/2tsp cayenne, 1tsp salt, 1tsp pepper, and mix -remove a wing from marinade, let it drip, then put in flour mixture, apply pressure and push flour into the wing, set aside until all are done -drop 4/5 wings in at a time. Cook until 175 and golden brown -pull out and salt -dip or paint your wings in your garlic parm sauce (if it gets cold and thick just microwave for 10 seconds) -enjoy !! #fyp #fypシ #wings #foodie #foodtok

Nutella cookie pie 🍪🤎✨ Ingredients: ¾ cup browned butter (160g) ⅔ cup brown sugar (140g) ¼ cup granulated sugar (50g) 1 egg 1 tbsp heavy cream (15g) 1 tsp vanilla 2 cups all-purpose flour (235g) ½ tsp baking soda (3g) ¼ tsp salt (1.5g) Chocolate chips (as much as you want) Nutella for the middle Extra chocolate chips on top Instructions: Brown the butter and let it cool slightly. In a bowl whisk together the browned butter, brown sugar, and granulated sugar until glossy. Add the egg, heavy cream, and vanilla and whisk until smooth. Fold in the flour, baking soda, and salt until just combined, then fold in the chocolate chips. Line a round cake pan or pie dish with parchment paper. Press a little over half of the cookie dough into the bottom and slightly up the sides. Spread a thick layer of Nutella in the center, then cover with the remaining cookie dough. Add extra chocolate chips on top. Bake at 175°C (350°F) for about 25–35 minutes, until the edges are golden and the center is still slightly soft. Let it cool a bit before slicing so the Nutella stays gooey. Enjoy✨ #baking #cookie #chocolate #اكسبلور #nutella

Crispy bacon, eggs cooked in bacon fat, and a sweet soy sauce glaze over rice. Simple, dangerous, and way too good. If you’re interested in Japanese home cooking, check out the link in my profile. Bacon and Egg Rice Bowl Ingredients — 1 serving ・Bacon: 120g ・Eggs: 2 ・Cooked rice: 1 serving (Sauce) ・Soy sauce: 2 tsp ・Mirin: 2 tsp ・Sugar: 1/2 tsp ・Grated garlic: 1/2 tsp (Toppings) ・Chopped green onion: as needed ・Black pepper: as needed Instructions ① Cut the bacon to your preferred thickness. ② Cook the bacon in a pan without oil until nicely browned. ③ Fry the eggs in the bacon fat. ④ Add the sauce ingredients and simmer for about 1 minute. ⑤ Serve over rice and finish with green onion and black pepper. You HAVE to try this Mapo Tofu from My Hero Academia! Bakugo nailed it Comment your thoughts on MHA and follow for more! Recipe - 1lb ground pork - 2-3 inch knob ginger - 6 cloves garlic  - 6 Birds Eye chilies  - 6 whole green onions - 1-2 tbsp ground Szechuan peppercorns - 1/3 cup shaoxing wine - 1/4 cup soy sauce  - 2 cups chicken stock - I like to use medium tofu for this. I know some people do silken but I like a little more texture  - 4 tbsp doubanjiang - 2 tbsp douchi - 2-3 tbsp sugar - 2 big pinches of salt and msg - 1tbsp cornstarch slurry - 1/4 cup lard Start by prepping your vegetables. Grate all of the ginger and garlic. Reserve half of the ginger for marinating the pork. Add ginger salt pepper and a splash each of soy sauce and shaoxing wine. Knead to combine.  Brown the pork in a wok then add in all of your vegetables, saute. Add in your pastes and peppercorns. Cook for a couple more minutes before adding chicken stock, soy sauce and shaoxing wine. Season here. I like to reduce this down by about 1/3 before adding the slurry. Allow to thicken then add in your tofu and green onions tops. Serve over rice and enjoy! @YOSUKATA Wok #bakugo #myheroacademia #animefood #mapotofu #fyp

Honey BBQ Chicken Tenders 🍯 Level up a boring cookout with these chicken tenders using @Sweet Baby Ray’s to make a sticky + sweet honey bbq glaze 😋 The sauce takes these to another level, so don’t skip it 🙂‍↔️ 👉 full detailed recipes in bio 🔗 — RECIPE: Chicken (Dry Brine): 6–8 tenders tossed with kosher salt, black pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika. Rest uncovered in the fridge for at least 2 hours (overnight is best). Seasoned Flour + Wet Batter: Mix AP flour, cornstarch, baking powder, salt, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, cayenne, black pepper + pinch MSG. Move ~⅓ to another bowl and whisk with cold sparkling water until thin pancake batter. Double Dredge: Mix 1–2 tbsp wet batter into dry flour for craggly bits. Coat chicken: wet → dry → wet → dry (press to stick). Fry: 350°F oil for 4–6 mins until golden + CWISPY (and 165F internal). Honey BBQ Glaze: Heat BBQ sauce (Sweet Baby Ray’s), honey, butter, vinegar, hot sauce until smooth + glossy. Season to taste. Toss or brush chicken with glaze while it’s still hot. Finish: Top with herbs, an extra drizzle of sauce, & serve with ranch. Enjoy friends :)  — #sbrpartner #honeybbq #chickentenders #friedchicken #easyrecipe

How to make the MOST FAMOUS bowl of ramen in the world 🍥 Comment some of your favorite fights in Naruto and follow for more! Pork broth - 6 pork legs - 6 pork knuckles Par boil for 10 minutes then rinse clean - 6 green onion - 1 white onion - 1 cup sliced ginger  - 1 half Fuji apple - water will vary depending on your pot I think mine was 7 or 8 liters Parboil you bones then rinse clean. Clean you pot really well then Fill with fresh water. Add the bones back in and boil 8-10 hours checking the water and skimming an impurities that float to the top. Once it’s a beautiful thick white stock, add in your aromatics and maybe a little water and boil for another 2 hours. Strain Tare recipe - 1/4 cup mirin  - 1/4 cup sake - 1.5 tbsp red miso - 1.5 tbsp black sesame miso (whatever you have is fine) - 1tbsp hondashi - 1 tbsp sesame seeds Cook down the mirin and sake until alcohol is cooked out before adding the other ingredients and cook for 3-5 minutes. Blend Chashu recipe - 2lb pork belly  - 1 cup dark soy - 2/3 mirin - 1/2 cup soy sauce - water until pork is almost submerged  Wrap your pork belly as tight as possible. Tie with kitchen twine. Sear on both sides then add in your braising liquid. Once it simmers cover and let cook 2-3 hours flipping halfway through. Let cool in the braising liquid then add to a ziplock bag and refrigerate overnight. Slice into disks and torch  Eggs - 3 large eggs - use the chashu braising liquid as the marinade (trust) Boil eggs for like 6:20 then immediately put in ice water. Combine your marinade and let them marinate for 12 hours or so Option aroma oil -slice ginger, garlic, and scallions. Pour 350F oil over them and let cool I soaked the bamboo shoots in the chashu braising liquid for about an hour but that’s optional #narutoramen #ramenlover #tonkotsu #animefood #fyp

Crockpot Chicken Pasta Ingredients • 2 lbs boneless skinless chicken breasts • 1 tsp salt • 1 tsp black pepper • 1 tsp garlic powder • 1 tsp onion powder • 1 tbsp Kinder’s Buttery Garlic & Herb Seasoning • 1 jar marinara sauce (24 oz) • 1 jar Alfredo sauce (15 oz) • 1 block cream cheese (8 oz) • 3 cloves garlic, minced • 1 bag shredded mozzarella cheese (8 oz) • Parsley flakes, for garnish Instructions 1. Add the chicken breasts to the crockpot and season with salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, and Kinder’s Buttery Garlic & Herb Seasoning. 2. Pour in the marinara sauce and Alfredo sauce. Add the cream cheese and minced garlic, then gently mix everything together. 3. Cover and cook on HIGH for 3 hours, or until the chicken is fully cooked and tender. 4. Remove the chicken, shred it with two forks, and return it to the crockpot. 5. Add the shredded mozzarella cheese and stir until the cheese is melted and everything is creamy and combined. 6. Top with parsley flakes and serve. #crockpotrecipes #chickenpasta #slowcookerrecipes #easyrecipes #comfortfood

#Creamy #Queso #Chicken Enchiladas 🌮🧀 Tender chicken #enchiladas smothered in creamy queso blanco and melty cheddar for bold #Tex-Mex flavor. Made with simple ingredients, this easy comfort dinner is perfect for busy family nights. Ingredients: 5 Burrito Size Flour Tortillas 2 ½ cup Shredded Chicken ½ Packet Taco Seasoning 1 cup Sour Cream 1 cup Cheddar Cheese shredded 2 tablespoons Chopped Green Chilies 10 ounces Diced Tomatoes with Green Chilies undrained 1 pounds Queso Blanco Velveeta cubed Instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the shredded chicken, taco seasoning, sour cream, shredded cheddar cheese, and chopped green chilies. Stir until everything is well mixed, then set the bowl aside. In a saucepan over medium-high heat, add the cubed Velveeta and the undrained can of diced tomatoes with green chilies. Stir occasionally until the cheese is completely melted and smooth. Lay the tortillas out on a flat surface. Spoon about ½ to ¾ cup of the chicken mixture into the center of each tortilla, then spread it slightly and roll it up to form a burrito. Repeat until all the tortillas are filled. Arrange the burritos side by side in a 9x13 casserole dish. Pour the warm queso evenly over the top of the burritos. Bake at 350°F for 20–25 minutes, until everything is heated through and bubbly. Serve hot and enjoy!

d: 5-minute bulgogi that doesn’t need to be pre-marinated! if you’re new here, welcome back to lazy girl dinners where i make easy asian-inspired meals the whole family loves. lazy girl dinners, ep 4: 5-minute bulgogi 🥩 what you need: 1.5-2 lbs thinly sliced beef brisket (or ground beef!) 1 onion, sliced ⅓ cup soy sauce 2 tbsp mirin 2 tbsp sugar (i used applesauce!) 1 tbsp sesame oil 1 tsp dashi powder how to make it: slice onion and sauté until fragrant add in thinly sliced beef mix sauce ingredients together and pour over beef close lid and let cook for 3 minutes or until fully cooked through garnish with sesame seeds and green onions serve over rice #lazygirldinners #familymeals #easymeal #easyrecipe #quickrecipes

📌 INGREDIENTS AND INSTRUCTIONS for 25-MINUTES CRISPY BEEF TACOS:  FULL RECIPE: LINK IN BIO 🔗  Follow @zaddysfavourites for more easy delicious recipes  Servings: 8 tacos 📋 INGREDIENTS:  ☑️ 500g [1 lb] Regular Minced/Ground Beef (20% fat)  ☑️ 1 Medium Onion, finely diced  ☑️ 1 tbsp Fresh Garlic, minced ☑️ 1 Large Bell Pepper, any colour  ☑️ ½ Hot Chili Pepper, optional, adjust to taste  ☑️ 2 tbsp Tomato Paste or Purée (Double Concentrate)  ☑️ 1 tbsp Worcestershire Sauce  ☑️ 1 tsp Honey  ☑️ 1 tsp Garlic Powder  ☑️ 1 tsp Smoked Paprika  ☑️ 1 tsp Cumin  ☑️ 1 tsp Dried Basil  ☑️ ½ tsp Fine Salt  ☑️ ½ tsp Black Pepper, ground Beef Broth ☑️ 120ml [½ cup] Hot Water  ☑️ 4g [0.20 oz] Beef Cube To Assemble ☑️ 200g [2 cups] Shredded Mozzarella  ☑️ 8 Mini Tortillas  ☑️ Sauce: Healthier Burger Sauce or Pink Sauce (recipe on website - link in bio) ☑️ Olive Oil or other Neutral Oil, for frying 🧑🏽‍🍳 INSTRUCTIONS:  1️⃣ Prep:  * Bring 500g (1 lb) regular ground beef (20% fat) to room temperature. * Mince 1 tbsp fresh garlic and ½ hot chili pepper together; set aside. * Chop 1 medium onion and 1 large bell pepper (any color) together; set aside. * Dissolve 4g (0.20 oz) beef bouillon cube into 120 ml (½ cup) hot water to make your broth; set aside. * Mix your preferred Burger Sauce or Pink Sauce (recipe on website - link in bio) in a small bowl and place it in the fridge to chill. 2️⃣ Cook the Beef * Heat a drizzle of olive oil or neutral oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the ground beef, spreading it into an even layer. Let it cook undisturbed until a deep brown crust forms, then break it apart and finish cooking. * Toss in the chopped onions and bell pepper. Sauté for 5 minutes until the vegetables soften. * Stir in the minced garlic and chili pepper, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant. * Push the meat mixture to the sides of the pan, clearing a circle in the centre. Add 2 tbsp double-concentrate tomato paste to the clearing. Stir and fry the paste continuously for 2 minutes until it darkens and turns fragrant, then mix it into the beef. * Stir in 1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce, 1 tsp honey, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp dried basil, ½ tsp fine salt, and ½ tsp ground black pepper. * Pour in the prepared beef broth. Simmer for 5 minutes until the liquid has mostly evaporated, leaving the meat rich and glossy. Transfer the beef mixture to a clean bowl. 3️⃣ Assemble & Fry * Wipe the skillet clean and heat a small amount of oil over medium heat. * Place a tortilla flat in the pan. Immediately scatter a layer of shredded mozzarella cheese and a generous scoop of the beef mixture over the entire surface. * Fry until the bottom of the tortilla is golden-brown and crispy, and the cheese is fully melted. * Fold the tortilla in half to enclose the filling, then transfer it to a wire rack to maintain its crispness while you repeat the process with the remaining tortillas and beef. 4️⃣ Serve * Serve hot alongside your chilled burger sauce or pink sauce. Enjoy! 🚨 Meal Prep Tip: To learn how to properly store and reheat this dish for later in the week, check Note 1 on the website - link in bio. ✅ family friendly dinner idea ✅ grab-n-go recipe ✅ high protein meal (see website for all the MACROS - link in bio) #tacos #beeftacos #beef #easyrecipes #familyfriendly

Cacio e Pepe🇮🇹🧀 Ingredients (Serves 2) • 250g spaghetti or tonnarelli • 120g Pecorino Romano, finely grated • 2 tsp freshly cracked black pepper • Pasta water, reserved Instructions 1. Grate the cheese:     Finely grate the Pecorino Romano.     The finer the cheese, the smoother the sauce. 2. Cook the pasta:     Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.     Cook the pasta until al dente.     Reserve at least 250ml of pasta water before draining. 3. Make the cheese cream:     Add a small amount of warm pasta water to the Pecorino Romano.     Whisk continuously.     Slowly add more water until you have a smooth, creamy sauce. 4. Toast the pepper:     Add the black pepper to a dry pan over medium heat.     Toast for 30–60 seconds until fragrant. 5. Build the sauce:     Add a splash of pasta water to the pan with the pepper.     Add the cooked pasta and toss well. 6. Combine:     Remove the pan from direct heat.     Add the Pecorino mixture and continue tossing.     Add more pasta water if needed until silky and glossy. 7. Serve:     Finish with extra Pecorino Romano.     Top with freshly cracked black pepper. #cacioepepesauce #cacioepepe #pastarecipes #dinnerideas #easyrecipes

d: Sheet Pan Chicken Taco Lettuce Boats I forget how ridiculously good this recipe is every single time I make it. Crispy taco chicken, chili-lime street corn, all the cold toppings, and somehow you still feel like a functioning adult afterward instead of needing a nap. And yes, I used queso fresco. Mind your business.                    Ingredients For the chicken: * 2½ lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs, chopped small * 2 packets taco seasoning * 1 teaspoon garlic powder * 1 teaspoon smoked paprika * Juice of 1 lime For the chili-lime corn: * 4 fresh ears of corn, kernels shaved off * ½ purple onion, diced * 1 tablespoon olive oil * 1 teaspoon chili powder * Salt to taste * Juice of ½–1 lime * ½ cup queso fresco * Fresh cilantro, chopped For serving: * Green leaf lettuce (sandwich cut) * Diced avocado * Pico de gallo * Jalapeño slices * Crema or Greek yogurt * Extra cilantro Instructions 1. Preheat oven to 425°F and line a sheet pan with parchment paper. 2. Toss chopped chicken with taco seasoning, garlic powder, smoked paprika, and lime juice. 3. Spread chicken onto one side of the sheet pan in an even layer. No oil needed. 4. Bake for 10 minutes. 5. Toss corn and purple onion with olive oil, chili powder, and salt. 6. Remove the pan, push chicken to one side, and add the corn mixture to the empty side. 7. Return to oven for another 10 minutes, then broil the last 3 minutes for crispy edges and charred corn. 8. Remove from oven and squeeze lime juice over the corn. Top with queso fresco and cilantro. 9. Assemble lettuce boats with chicken, street corn, avocado, pico, jalapeños, crema, and extra cilantro #taco #dinner #recipe #sheetpandinner #chicken

Viral egg ema datshi✨ Ema datshi is the national dish of Bhutan and it literally translates to “chillies and cheese”. It’s creamy, spicy and highly comforting! When I found out that it could be made with eggs, I knew I had to try it asap. So so good..absolute must try!  Ingredients I used: - 1 tsp butter  - 1 to 2 cloves garlic, finely chopped  - 1 to 2 green chillies, chopped  - 2 slices of cheese  - water as needed  - 1 tsp homemade chilli oil (check out the linked recipe) - 2 eggs  - some more chilli oil for garnish  #emadatshi #eggs #eggemadatshi #bhutanesefood

Korea’s moving day food  Seafood tray jajangmyeon 🧡 Ingredients (2 servings)  1 onion 2 green onions 1 squid Shrimp, scallops 2 servings Chinese-style noodles 100g black bean paste (product link in bio) 💙 Directions  1. Thinly slice the onion and green onions. 2. Cut the squid into bite-sized pieces and prepare the shrimp, scallops, or any other seafood you like. 3. Heat some oil in a pan and cook the onion and green onions over low heat until fragrant. 4. Add the black bean paste and stir-fry for a minute. Add a little water if needed, depending on your preferred sauce consistency. 5. Cook the noodles according to the package instructions. Rinse under cold water to remove excess starch and drain well. 6. Add the noodles to the sauce and stir-fry until evenly coated. 7. Sprinkle in some gochugaru, if desired. 🔖 Save this recipe for your next meal  🥰 Like, follow & comment if you enjoyed  .  .  . .  . . #koreanfood #easyrecipes #comfortfood #jajangmyeon #quickrecipes
